claims.claim_text: The CKA blueprint gives significant weight to Services & Networking and Troubleshooting.
claims.rationale: The official CNCF CKA page lists Services & Networking at 20% and Troubleshooting at 30% of the exam.

claims.claim_text: Each pod gets its own unique cluster-wide IP address, so Kubernetes does not rely on host port mapping for pod-to-pod reachability.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes network model says each pod gets a unique cluster-wide IP and pods communicate directly without NAT.

claims.claim_text: The module states that all cluster DNS resolution always goes through CoreDNS pods in kube-system.
claims.anchor_text: All cluster DNS resolution goes through CoreDNS pods in kube-system
claims.suggested_rewrite: In default Kubernetes setups, cluster DNS queries usually go to the `kube-dns` Service, which is commonly backed by CoreDNS pods in `kube-system`
claims.rationale: Upstream docs describe the common CoreDNS-backed `kube-dns` pattern, but not an absolute universal rule for every cluster.

claims.claim_text: NetworkPolicies are additive: if any applicable policy allows traffic, that traffic is allowed.
claims.rationale: The upstream NetworkPolicy docs explicitly state that policies do not conflict and are additive.

claims.claim_text: The module says that having any NetworkPolicy automatically creates default deny behavior.
claims.anchor_text: having ANY policy creates default deny
claims.suggested_rewrite: a pod is only isolated for ingress or egress when an applicable policy covers that direction
claims.rationale: Isolation is direction-specific and only applies when a policy selects the pod and covers that traffic direction.

claims.claim_text: Service IPs are virtual, kube-proxy programs packet-handling rules for them, and kube-proxy IPVS mode is deprecated in Kubernetes v1.35.
claims.rationale: The upstream virtual IPs reference documents Service VIP behavior, kube-proxy rule programming, and IPVS deprecation in v1.35.

further_reading.title: Debug Services
further_reading.why_relevant: Walks through the canonical endpoint, selector, and service-port troubleshooting flow used throughout this module.

further_reading.title: DNS for Services and Pods
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains Kubernetes DNS names, search paths, and service-discovery behavior that underpins the DNS checks in this module.

further_reading.title: Network Policies
further_reading.why_relevant: Defines additive policy semantics and ingress/egress isolation rules needed to reason about default-deny behavior correctly.

notes: Excluded diagrams, code blocks, tables, quiz content, and hands-on exercise steps per pipeline rules; seeded only prose/callout claims that need inline support or correction.
